VENDOR DUE DILIGENCE QUESTIONNAIRE — COMPLETED RESPONSES
Vendor: BetaCloud Technologies Ltd.
Engagement: ENG-2026-047
Completed: March 12, 2026
Point of Contact: Sarah Kim, VP Compliance (sarah.kim@betacloud.io)

===========================================================================
SECTION A — FINANCIAL STABILITY
===========================================================================

Q1. Provide your most recent audited financial statements.
A: BetaCloud's audited financial statements for FY2025 (ended December 31,
2025) were completed by Deloitte & Touche on February 28, 2026. Revenue was
$42M (up 38% YoY), gross margin 71%, and the company holds $18M in cash with
no long-term debt. Copies of the full audited statements are attached as
Exhibit A.

Q2. What is your current runway and burn rate?
A: At current burn rate of approximately $1.1M per month (inclusive of
headcount growth planned through Q2 2026), the company has approximately
16 months of runway. We closed a $25M Series B in October 2025 (led by
Sequoia with participation from existing investors). Term sheet for a
$15M venture debt facility from Silicon Valley Bank is in final negotiation.

Q3. Describe any material litigation, regulatory actions, or financial
    restatements in the last 3 years.
A: There are no material litigation matters, regulatory enforcement actions,
or financial restatements to disclose. A minor employment matter (wrongful
termination claim, $85K demand) was settled in June 2024 with no admission
of liability. We have disclosed this in the attached Exhibit B (Legal Summary).

===========================================================================
SECTION B — OPERATIONAL RESILIENCE
===========================================================================

Q4. What is your documented uptime SLA and how is it measured?
A: Our enterprise SLA guarantees 99.95% monthly uptime, measured from our
primary AWS us-east-1 region. Downtime is calculated on a 1-minute resolution
basis using Route53 health checks and confirmed by PagerDuty. Historical
uptime data is available on our Trust Portal at trust.betacloud.io.
Over the trailing 12 months (April 2025 – March 2026) actual uptime was
99.97%.

Q5. Describe your disaster recovery and business continuity capabilities.
A: BetaCloud operates active-active across AWS us-east-1 and us-west-2.
Our documented RTO is 15 minutes and RPO is 5 minutes, validated in our
most recent DR test (February 5, 2026). A full DR runbook (DR-RB-v4) and
BCP (BCP-2026-01) are attached as Exhibit C. The DR test was witnessed by
an independent auditor from A-LIGN and the sign-off report is included.

Q6. How do you manage third-party and supply chain risk?
A: We maintain a Vendor Risk Register (updated quarterly) covering all
critical and significant third parties. Third parties are tiered (Critical /
Significant / Standard) using an annual risk scoring methodology. Critical
vendors (AWS, Stripe, Twilio, Okta) are reviewed annually with on-site or
virtual assessments. We require SOC 2 Type II or ISO 27001 from all Critical
and Significant vendors. The current vendor register excerpt is Exhibit D.

===========================================================================
SECTION C — DATA HANDLING AND PRIVACY
===========================================================================

Q7. How is customer data encrypted in transit and at rest?
A: All data in transit is encrypted using TLS 1.3 (minimum TLS 1.2 for
legacy clients with a deprecation roadmap). Data at rest in RDS, S3, and
DynamoDB is encrypted using AES-256 with AWS KMS customer-managed keys
(CMKs). CMKs are rotated annually; rotation logs are available in CloudTrail.
Encryption scope includes backups, snapshots, and all replication traffic.

Q8. Describe your data retention and deletion practices.
A: Customer data is retained for the duration of the contract plus 90 days
post-termination (configurable to 30 days upon written request). After the
retention window, data is securely deleted using NIST 800-88 compliant
procedures. Deletion certificates are issued within 30 days of request.
Our full data retention schedule (DRS-2026-01) is Exhibit E.

Q9. Do you maintain a sub-processor list and how do you manage sub-processor risk?
A: Yes. Our current sub-processor list is publicly available at
trust.betacloud.io/sub-processors (last updated March 1, 2026). Changes to
sub-processors are communicated to customers with 30 days notice via email
and in-app notification. All sub-processors are subject to a written DPA
and an annual security review.

===========================================================================
SECTION D — GOVERNANCE
===========================================================================

Q10. Describe your security governance structure.
A: BetaCloud's CISO (David Park) reports to the CEO and provides monthly
security updates to the executive team and quarterly briefings to the Board's
Audit Committee. We maintain an Information Security Committee with
representation from Engineering, Legal, Finance, and Product that meets monthly.

Q11. What compliance certifications does your organization hold?
A: Current certifications:
- SOC 2 Type II (AICPA, Security + Availability + Confidentiality criteria),
  examination period January 1 – December 31, 2025, report issued February 20,
  2026, clean opinion. Report available under NDA upon execution of a
  standard NDA form.
- ISO 27001:2022 certified by BSI Group, certificate number IS 901234,
  valid through March 2027.
- GDPR: we have appointed a DPO (external, Fieldfisher LLP) and maintain
  Article 30 records of processing activities.

Q12. Describe your employee security training program.
A: All employees complete role-based security awareness training at onboarding
and annually thereafter using KnowBe4 platform. Completion rate for the 2025
annual cycle was 98.7% (remainder completed within 30-day cure period).
Engineering staff complete additional secure coding training (OWASP Top 10 +
internal threat modeling workshop) annually. Phishing simulation exercises
are run quarterly; 2025 click-through rate was 2.3%.
